subject: eddy-lazzarin

sectors_excited.name: Stablecoins & onchain payments
sectors_excited.why: The clearest product-market fit in crypto — dollars moving onchain at scale. Improving US regulatory clarity plus fintech and institutional integration can turn settlement rails into a mainstream payments layer.

sectors_excited.name: AI x crypto
sectors_excited.why: As autonomous AI agents proliferate, crypto supplies the primitives they need: programmable payments, verifiable identity and proof-of-personhood, and provenance for content and compute. Decentralized compute and data markets are a natural fit.

sectors_excited.name: Scaling & infrastructure (L2s, zero-knowledge, account abstraction)
sectors_excited.why: Lower fees, better UX, and cryptographic verifiability are prerequisites for consumer-scale apps. ZK is becoming a foundational primitive far beyond just scaling.

sectors_excited.name: DePIN (decentralized physical infrastructure)
sectors_excited.why: Token incentives can bootstrap real-world networks — compute, wireless, energy, storage — faster than incumbents while aligning early users as owners.

sectors_concerned.name: Memecoins & pure speculation
sectors_concerned.why: Speculative churn crowds out durable building, degrades UX and trust, and risks defining the industry by its most extractive corner.

sectors_concerned.name: Low-float / high-FDV token structures
sectors_concerned.why: Tiny circulating supply with large future unlocks distorts price discovery and leaves later participants holding the overhang — a design misalignment the industry should fix.

risk_regime.why: Structurally constructive on the building and adoption cycle: improving US regulatory clarity, maturing infrastructure, and real usage in stablecoins and onchain apps. This is a view on the long-term innovation trajectory, not a short-term price call.

notes: My logged record here is empty, so every item is 'extrapolated' from how I reason as a16z crypto's CTO — long-term, builder- and infrastructure-focused — not from any specific dated statement; that is why there are no verbatim citations. I am deliberately not naming individual tokens: with no grounded record, assigning conviction to specific tokens would be manufacturing a view (and carries obvious conflict-of-interest as an investor). Views as of 2025-03-31.
